zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II於1479年出生在英國肯特澤文亞可斯時，他的父親Sir John Lennard20歲，母親Lady Mary Anne Byrd21歲。他於1501年與Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston在英國英格蘭肯特澤文亞可斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭肯特被列為high sheriff。他去世於1554年7月在英國肯特格拉福森德享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特格拉福森德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II于1479年出生在英国肯特澤文亞可斯时，他的父亲Sir John Lennard20岁，母亲Lady Mary Anne Byrd21岁。他于1501年与Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston在英国英格兰肯特澤文亞可斯结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他在英国英格兰肯特被列为high sheriff。他去世于1554年7月在英国肯特格拉福森德享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特格拉福森德。

nameMeanings.name: Lennard,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Leonard  .
Americanized form of German   Lennert   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Leonard
meanings: English; French (Léonard); Walloon (mainly Léonard): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements leo ‘lion’ (a late addition to the vocabulary of ancient Germanic name elements, taken from Latin) + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’, which was taken to England by the Normans. A Christian saint of this name, who is supposed to have lived in the 6th century, but about whom nothing is known except for a largely fictional life dating from half a millennium later, was popular throughout Europe in the early Middle Ages and was regarded as the patron of peasants and horses. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Leonardo , Polish, Slovenian, etc. Lenart or Lenard , and probably also their derivatives. Compare Larned , Learned , and Yenor .
Irish (Fermanagh): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Fhionáin or of Langan .
German: variant of   Leonhard  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: II,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Kent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from the county of Kent. The surname is also established in Ireland, where English bearers of this name settled in Meath, in the 13th century. The name has been Gaelicized as Ceannt.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II was born in 1479, in Sevenoaks, Kent, England, his father, Sir John Lennard, was 20 and his mother, Lady Mary Anne Byrd, was 21. He married Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston in 1501, in Sevenoaks, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. His occupation is listed as high sheriff in Kent, England, United Kingdom. He died in July 1554, in Gravesend, Kent, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Gravesend, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II est né en 1479, à Sevenoaks, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Lennard, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Lady Mary Anne Byrd, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston en 1501, à Sevenoaks, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en juillet 1554, à Gravesend, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Gravesend, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II 1479 in Sevenoaks, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Lennard 20 und seine Mutter Lady Mary Anne Byrd 21. Er hat 1501 in Sevenoaks, Kent, England, United Kingdom Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als high sheriff in Kent, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb im Juli 1554 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Gravesend, Kent, England und wurde in Gravesend, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II è nato il nel 1479 nel Sevenoaks, Kent, England, il suo padre, Sir John Lennard, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Lady Mary Anne Byrd, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston nel 1501, nel Sevenoaks, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel luglio 1554, nel Gravesend, Kent, England, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Gravesend, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent IIが1479年にイギリスケントセベノアクスで生まれた時，父Sir John Lennardは20歳，母Lady Mary Anne Byrdは21歳であった。1501年にイギリスイギリスケントセベノアクスでLady Katherine Elizabeth Westonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスケントでの職業はhigh sheriffと記録されている。彼は1554年7月にイギリスケントグラベセンドで75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントグラベセンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II(이)가 1479년 영국 켄트 세베노크스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Lennard(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Mary Anne Byrd(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1501년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 세베노크스에서 Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 high sheriff이었다. 그는 1554년 7월 영국 켄트 그레이베센드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 그레이베센드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II nasceu em 1479, em Sevenoaks, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Lennard, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Lady Mary Anne Byrd, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston em 1501, em Sevenoaks, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como high sheriff em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em julho de 1554, em Gravesend, Kent, Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Gravesend, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II родился в 1479 г. в местности сивеноакс, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Lennard было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Mary Anne Byrd, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston в 1501 г. в местности сивеноакс, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери.  Он работал по профессии high sheriff в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер июль 1554 г. в местности гравесенд, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности гравесенд, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II nació en 1479, en Sevenoaks, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Lennard, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Lady Mary Anne Byrd, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston en 1501, en Sevenoaks, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era high sheriff en Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en julio de 1554, en Gravesend, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Gravesend, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
